subject: Geography
title: Volcanic Islands
description: A quick, practical guide to Volcanic Islands.

hook.text: In 1963, an undersea eruption near Iceland created a brand-new island called Surtsey.

buildup.text: Volcanic islands form when magma breaks through the ocean floor and piles above the waves.

discovery.text: Hawaii, Iceland, and the Canary Islands all rose from volcanic eruptions on the seafloor.

twist.text: Surtsey is a living lab—scientists watch as life colonizes bare rock from scratch.

climax.text: New volcanic islands are rare but remind us that Earth is still actively building.

punchline.text: The ocean floor can birth a new country overnight.

quiz.question: When was the island of Surtsey created?
